body: {"answer": "Glaucoma is a group of diseases that can damage the eye's optic nerve and result in vision loss and blindness. It typically occurs when the fluid pressure inside the eyes rises, leading to optic nerve damage. Early treatment can often protect the eyes against serious vision loss. There is no cure for glaucoma, but treatments such as prescription eyedrops and surgery can help manage the condition. Risk factors include age (particularly over 60), family history, and certain ethnic backgrounds. Regular eye exams are crucial for early detection and management."}
